name: disk_seek
title: Storage access latency
short_description: Time to complete a single small random read from storage.

structured_text.description: Issues small reads to random positions one at a time, waiting for each to finish before starting the next. With no overlap to hide the delay, the test exposes the true time it takes storage to serve a single scattered request.
structured_text.metric_explanation: The score is the average time to complete one random access, reported in microseconds.
structured_text.value_interpretation: Lower is better. Low access latency keeps a system feeling instant, since many short waits add up across everyday use.
structured_text.reasoning: Throughput hides behind queues of requests, but single access latency is what the user actually feels when an app or file responds, so it complements the random read test.

domain_gaming.explanation: Hitching during asset streaming often traces back to access latency.

domain_office.explanation: Everyday responsiveness depends on how quickly small files respond.

domain_software_development.explanation: Tools that stat and open many small files are sensitive to access latency.

domain_graphics_design.explanation: Loading small assets and previews benefits from low latency.

domain_video_editing.explanation: Media playback is more throughput bound than latency bound.

domain_3d_rendering.explanation: Gathering many small asset files is affected by access latency.

domain_audio_production.explanation: Triggering samples on demand needs low storage latency to avoid dropouts.

domain_cad_engineering.explanation: Opening projects made of many small references is latency sensitive.

domain_scientific_computing.explanation: Random access into large data files is gated by latency.

domain_ai_training.explanation: Random record access during shuffling is affected by latency.

domain_ai_inference.explanation: Loading model pieces on demand depends on storage latency.

domain_data_science.explanation: Point lookups into large stores are latency bound.

domain_server_virt.explanation: Database response times are tightly coupled to storage access latency.
